message.content:
Imagine if you had a big pile of sand that you kept adding more sand to. At first, it would look like a small, lumpy hill. But as you keep adding more sand, it would eventually start looking more like a smooth mound or a round shape. This happens because all the sand wants to settle into a shape where everything balances out nicely.

Planets are like super huge piles of sand, but instead of sand, they have lots of rocks, dirt, and gas. Because they are so big, the force called gravity pulls everything towards the center. Gravity is like an invisible hand that wants to make everything as close to that center as possible. The best way to do that is by being round, kind of like a ball.

When planets are small, they might be a bit lumpy. But when they get big, gravity makes them nice and round, like a ball, because that's the most balanced shape. So, bigger planets end up looking round because gravity is really strong and wants to make them smooth and even.
